name: OSM graph -> drivable geometry (Isaac demo only)
role: Turn OSMnx road centerlines into a flat collision/visual mesh for the Isaac EXECUTION/DEMO world. Training does NOT need this (the kinematic env drives on the graph directly) — it exists only so the physics demo cars have a real SF surface to drive on.
decision: Build it for the demo only, and keep it cheap: flat ground plane + OSMnx centerlines buffered to lane-width ribbons (shapely buffer -> triangulate -> trimesh -> OBJ -> USD via MeshConverter with a TRIANGLE collision approximation). Do NOT attempt faithful 3D multi-lane junctions. If the demo box slips, this can be dropped and you fall back to the kinematic-env render.
outputs: road-ribbon mesh OBJ/STL -> USD with collision (triangle approximation, not convex hull)
aligned so the kinematic-env coordinates match the Isaac world frame

difficulty_tiers.scope: flat plane only; cars follow the kinematic plan, local camera. Enough for a tight hero shot.
buffer+extrude centerlines into a real road ribbon mesh laid on the plane.

alternatives: OSM2World / blender-osm to generate OBJ/glTF, clean in Blender
skip geometry entirely and render the demo from the kinematic env on a deck.gl SF basemap (fallback)
risks: Isaac imported-USD colliders dropping in some 2025 builds -> verify contacts
coordinate-frame alignment between kinematic-env and Isaac world
do not let mesh fidelity eat the hackathon — it's demo polish, not the RL
